body_txt: The stereotypical way for runaways and Hobos alike to carry their belongings: wrap a bunch of objects in a bedsheet or large kerchief, tie it to a stick and sling it over your shoulder. The cloth of the bindle itself will usually be depicted as polka-dotted. In hobo slang during and after The Great Depression , someone who carried a Bindle Stick around was referred to as a "bindlestiff". Even after the Bindle Stick usage in real life virtually disappeared in The '50s due to availability of backpacks and other more convenient carrying methods, the usage of Bindle Sticks in media remained. Compare the Shopping Cart of Homelessness , a modern urban counterpart to this trope.
